title: trichilia trifolia subsp. palmeri
intro: Trichilia trifolia subsp. palmeri, also known as Palmer's Trichilia, is a subspecies of the Trichilia trifolia plant, a species of tree in the family Meliaceae native to Central America and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and exhibit antioxidant activity, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Trichilia trifolia subsp. palmeri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
